Results for VDBA Scaling Analysis

O.W.

2025-11-10

Methods

  • 37 species datasets of raw tri-axial accelerometer data.
  • Formatted to standard data structure
  • Outliers manually removed through visual inspection and clipping
  • Smoothed outliers with rolling mean (to remove unreasonable spikes)
  • Generated dynamic VDBA by removing static acceleration (either as a rolling average of 10 seconds of VDBA for continuous data or as the average of the burst)
  • Separated instances into active and inactive categories such that all instances with a rolling standard deviation. Everything above 0.05 VDBA was classed as “active”
  • Summarised max and mean VDBA per individual.

Individual Analysis

Where the masses of the exact individual are known. Per species plot and then all known individuals on the same graph.

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Single system analysis

Where exact masses are not included (species averages) but all data is collected using the same accelerometer sampling protocol.Repeated for all Clemente datasets and for all Buchmann datasets. # V1: Axivity (Clemente)

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Run the stats, mean then max. (Silenced because non-significant again)

V2: Buchmann

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Stats displayed here because the maxVDBA was significant for LogMass.

# Stats -------------------------------------------------------------------
buchmann_model <- lmer(log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + (1 | Dataset), data = buchmann_summaries)
summary(buchmann_model)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + (1 | Dataset)
##    Data: buchmann_summaries
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -18.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -3.12040 -0.44596  0.00121  0.63492  2.43733 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Dataset  (Intercept) 0.01235  0.1111  
##  Residual             0.03658  0.1913  
## Number of obs: 99, groups:  Dataset, 8
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                           Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                0.59379    0.61329  7.91196   0.968   0.3616    
## LogMass                   -0.19192    0.11472  8.17163  -1.673   0.1321    
## thresholdall              -0.26175    0.49425 87.36215  -0.530   0.5977    
## thresholdinactive         -2.41104    0.53555 87.76646  -4.502 2.06e-05 ***
## LogMass:thresholdall       0.03893    0.09388 87.36215   0.415   0.6794    
## LogMass:thresholdinactive  0.25838    0.10137 87.85688   2.549   0.0125 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                (Intr) LogMss thrshldl thrshldn LgMss:thrshldl
## LogMass        -0.996                                        
## thresholdll    -0.403  0.408                                 
## thrshldnctv    -0.367  0.371  0.461                          
## LgMss:thrshldl  0.401 -0.409 -0.996   -0.460                 
## LgMss:thrshldn  0.367 -0.374 -0.461   -0.995    0.463
buchmann_active_data <- buchmann_summaries %>% dplyr::filter(threshold == "active")
buchmann_max_model <- lmer(log10(maxVDBA) ~ LogMass + (1 | Dataset), data = buchmann_active_data)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
summary(buchmann_max_model)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: log10(maxVDBA) ~ LogMass + (1 | Dataset)
##    Data: buchmann_active_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -16
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.2288 -0.5743  0.0387  0.6025  2.0956 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Dataset  (Intercept) 0.000    0.0000  
##  Residual             0.032    0.1789  
## Number of obs: 38, groups:  Dataset, 8
## 
## Fixed effects:
##             Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.43792    0.32687 36.00000   4.399 9.27e-05 ***
## LogMass     -0.23274    0.06209 36.00000  -3.749 0.000624 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##         (Intr)
## LogMass -0.996
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

All species pooled

All species, with approximate/average masses.

# select only the big datasets
# all_volumes <- lapply(species_list, function(x){
#   vol <- fread(file.path(x, paste0(basename(x), "_volume.csv")))
#   vol$Species <- basename(x)
#   vol
# })
# all_volumes <- rbindlist(all_volumes)
# big_volumes <- all_volumes[all_volumes$volume > 24, ]
# big_volumes_data <- vdba_stuff1 %>% dplyr::filter(!Name %in% maxxed_species)
# 
# vdba_stuff1 <- big_volumes_data

vdba_stuff1 <- vdba_stuff1 %>%
  select(ID, threshold, meanVDBA, minVDBA, maxVDBA, Name, LogMass, Frequency, DeviceAttachment) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!Name == "Chakravarty_Meerkat")

# summarise by dataset
vdba_stuff1_summarised <- vdba_stuff1 %>% group_by(Name, threshold, LogMass, DeviceAttachment) %>%
   summarise(meanVDBA = mean(meanVDBA),
             maxVDBA = mean(maxVDBA),
             minVDBA = mean(minVDBA)
             )
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Name', 'threshold', 'LogMass'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
# Plots ------------------------------------------------------------------------
p4 <- ggplot(vdba_stuff1_summarised, aes(x = LogMass, y = log10(meanVDBA), shape = threshold)) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = Name)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = fave_colours) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() + # reset the colour scale
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", aes(colour = threshold), se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = threshold_cols) +
  my_theme() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p5 <- ggplot(vdba_stuff1_summarised %>% dplyr::filter(threshold == "active"), aes(x = LogMass, y = log10(maxVDBA), shape = threshold)) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = Name)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = fave_colours) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() + # reset the colour scale
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", aes(colour = threshold), se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = threshold_cols) +
  my_theme() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


p4 + p5
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# stats ------------------------------------------------------------------------
all_model <- lmer(log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + Frequency + (1 | Name) + (1|DeviceAttachment), data = vdba_stuff1)
summary(all_model)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + Frequency + (1 | Name) +  
##     (1 | DeviceAttachment)
##    Data: vdba_stuff1
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -345
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.3316 -0.5678  0.0444  0.7070  3.1116 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups           Name        Variance  Std.Dev. 
##  Name             (Intercept) 5.333e-02 2.309e-01
##  DeviceAttachment (Intercept) 8.808e-10 2.968e-05
##  Residual                     3.392e-02 1.842e-01
## Number of obs: 920, groups:  Name, 35; DeviceAttachment, 6
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                             Estimate Std. Error         df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)               -8.041e-01  2.195e-01  3.429e+01  -3.663 0.000833 ***
## LogMass                    4.924e-02  4.458e-02  3.555e+01   1.105 0.276745    
## thresholdall              -2.817e-01  6.892e-02  8.819e+02  -4.087 4.76e-05 ***
## thresholdinactive         -1.320e+00  7.045e-02  8.826e+02 -18.742  < 2e-16 ***
## Frequency                  5.383e-04  1.781e-03  3.148e+01   0.302 0.764414    
## LogMass:thresholdall       1.915e-02  1.576e-02  8.819e+02   1.215 0.224660    
## LogMass:thresholdinactive  4.818e-02  1.619e-02  8.829e+02   2.976 0.003004 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                (Intr) LogMss thrshldl thrshldn Frqncy LgMss:thrshldl
## LogMass        -0.938                                               
## thresholdll    -0.157  0.173                                        
## thrshldnctv    -0.152  0.166  0.489                                 
## Frequency      -0.425  0.145  0.000   -0.001                        
## LgMss:thrshldl  0.153 -0.177 -0.977   -0.478    0.000               
## LgMss:thrshldn  0.147 -0.169 -0.475   -0.977    0.001  0.487

And then only the files where the system didn’t max out.

## LgMss:thrshldn  0.467 -0.478 -0.484   -0.978    0.495

And then only the sepcies that didnt max and were on a collar.

vdba_stuff3_summarised <- vdba_stuff1_summarised %>% dplyr::filter(Name %in% range_species & DeviceAttachment == "Collar")

p9 <- ggplot(vdba_stuff3_summarised, aes(x = LogMass, y = log10(meanVDBA), shape = threshold)) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = Name)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = fave_colours) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() + # reset the colour scale
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", aes(colour = threshold), se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = threshold_cols) +
  my_theme() + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p10 <- ggplot(vdba_stuff3_summarised %>% dplyr::filter(threshold == "active"), aes(x = LogMass, y = log10(maxVDBA), shape = threshold)) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = Name)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = fave_colours) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() + # reset the colour scale
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", aes(colour = threshold), se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = threshold_cols) +
  my_theme()  + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

(p9 + p10) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        legend.text = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

all_model <- lmer(log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + (1 | Name), data = vdba_stuff3_summarised)
summary(all_model)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + (1 | Name)
##    Data: vdba_stuff3_summarised
##

##

## 
## Random effects:

## Number of obs: 68, groups:  Name, 23

## (Intercept)               -0.56980    0.24119 38.53975  -2.362   0.0233 *  
## LogMass                    0.00189    0.05193 38.53975   0.036   0.9712    
## thresholdall              -0.45928    0.22950 40.50440  -2.001   0.0521 .  
## thresholdinactive         -1.41719    0.23047 40.57266  -6.149 2.76e-07 ***
## LogMass:thresholdall       0.06465    0.04941 40.50440   1.308   0.1981    
## LogMass:thresholdinactive  0.07020    0.04984 40.64334   1.409   0.1666    
## ---

##

##                (Intr) LogMss thrshldl thrshldn LgMss:thrshldl
## LogMass        -0.978

## thrshldnctv    -0.474  0.463  0.498

## LgMss:thrshldn  0.461 -0.472 -0.485   -0.978    0.496

Find the active minutes per day

Are large animals more or less active generally?

all_minutes_files <- list.files(file.path(base_path, "AccelerometerData"), recursive = TRUE, pattern = "*_active_minutes\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

minute_data <- lapply(all_minutes_files, function(x){
  dat <- fread(x) %>% na.omit()
  dat$Name <- basename(dirname(x))
  dat
})
minute_data <- rbindlist(minute_data, fill = TRUE)

dat <- merge(vdba_stuff1, minute_data, by = c("ID", "Name"))

# plot this
ggplot(dat, aes(x = LogMass, y = prop_active, colour = Name, group = 1)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation:
## colour.
## ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in
##   the data.
## ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical
##   variable into a factor?
